Are you excited for this? Yeah! Are you nervous for this? Uh-huh. I seriously get so nervous. Anyway, it's funny, people always ask me where I speak. Like, do you get nervous? I'm like, yes, every single time. Every rest of my life, I get super scared. And as soon as you step on the mat and you shake hands, it goes away. As soon as you step on stage, you see all the audience goes away. But I still get nervous every single time. So I think that's good, hopefully. It is. That or I'm just weird. So, all right. So I'm going to be holly for the next little bit. I'm going to try talking your accent. Just kidding, I'm not really going to do that at all. I don't think you have an accent. So it's going to be fun. All right. Any questions before you start it? No. Crap. Well, here we go. What's up, everybody? My name is Holly. Welcome to the webinar today. You guys decided to be here? And what happens is when we don't take care of ourselves, then our family suffers and becomes this really bad cycle that leads a lot of people to depression, to anger, to frustration. And a lot of times it ends in bad marriages and a whole bunch of other things. And so my goal with this is to help you so that spiral never happens. Now, one thing before I get into this, I want to tell you guys my story just so you guys, we have some connection before we jump too much into this. So my name is Holly and I was just like a lot of you guys. In fact, before I became a mom, I went to school, I had a career and things were good and I enjoyed that part of it. I felt a lot of significance and love and connection from the work, the work I was doing, stuff like that. And then my husband, I decided we should have kids. And I was so excited because I wanted to be that mom and I had this vision in my head that was going to look like, you guys remember the vision of that? How exciting. I'm going to be a mom. I was so excited for that. And then the day came where I became a mom. I saw my beautiful baby and it was so cool and so exciting and left work. I remember sitting there with this baby wondering like, what am I supposed to do all day? They're sleeping. And so I'd sit there bored and confused and I would say what to do and kind of this weird phase where I didn't know what to do or how to do it. And then I go to bed at night and then they wake up and they scream all night. And it was like, what in the world? How many of you guys are curious, you're already in that transition point right now where you're becoming a mom and you're leaving either a career or school or something, but you're kind of the spice right? I don't really know how to take this or what to do with it. How many of you guys feel right now? It's kind of weird, right? Like I totally emphasize with that. And so another weird thing is like after I left my work, I disconnected from all of these people who are my friends. Like they were still working and doing stuff, having conversations and I was at home by myself and I felt lonely. My husband was at work and it was just this weird thing. I had this baby and I loved him or her. I loved him. He was so cute, but I didn't have anyone to talk to you and it was lonely. And for a lot of people, that's where depression starts and I get it. I went through that as well. And so for me, I was like, I got to figure out something. I need to get some friends and things to do. And I had a friend and she messaged me one day and said, I'm not going to email you anymore. I was like, if you don't email me, I'm going to lose my mind. She's like, no, I'm becoming a blogger. You should blog to a blog together. And I didn't know what that meant. I was like, what is a blog? This is 10 years ago now. It's supposed to be four blogs. We're cool. She's like, we should start a blog. And so I know what that meant, but it was better than losing my friendships. I started this blog and I started just telling funny stories about my kids. And I ran this blog for X amount of years and it was really fun because I would just tell all the crazy things my kids did. I'd talk about this and talk about that and it was really fun and built connection. But the weird things as I got bigger, or excuse me, as my kids got bigger, they started not liking the blog. I remember I would talk about their tantrums when they were two-year-old and it was really, really funny. By talking about tantrums, they're nine-year-old. It's really, really awkward because their friends and their teachers have read those things and they come back and it was kind of awkward. Also, one day I'm not going to lie, one creepy thing kind of happened. I was at the park with my kids and my three-year-old ran off and some stranger I didn't know, said, hey, Holly, Ryan just ran over there, you should go get him. And I was like, how do you know my name and my kid's name? And so I decided I can't keep just blogging the blog. And so I made this transition. I'm going to try, I'm going to make a new kind of, I don't know what kind of blog to make. But I had a friend who was selling a craft blog, had a craft blog and was trying to get rid of it. And I looked and I was like, I'm not crafty at all, but be kind of fun and maybe I'll become craft, I don't know, so I bought this craft blog from her and it came in my blog. And some of you guys are from my blog, obviously that's why you're probably here. And we have this huge craft blog that was blown up. It's become an amazing business force, a really cool thing and it's helped me to connect people all around the world. In fact, all my best friends have come from my blog and come from the connections I met because of it. And so that's some of my backstory and kind of why we do this kind of thing. Now the one thing I want to stress though is a lot of people that hear me talk about that, like, oh, well you're a crafty super mom, that's why you're able to do this. I'm not crafty. In fact, that leads me to the very first part of my presentation here, where I'm going to tell you guys the truth about the Pinterest moms. Okay, so how many of you guys have been on Pinterest once or twice, you've seen the most amazing things and you're like, man, that, wait, let me see, that blanket is so white. If I had that in my house, my kids would have ketchup and chocolate all over it. Like, it would be horrible, right? Okay, this is the truth about it. Most of the amazing Pinterest moms actually are running businesses. Okay, I run a business. In fact, I have a studio that takes amazing pictures of stuff and we post them on Pinterest because it's a business and we actually make money from it. But guess what? That stuff's not actually in my house. Okay, it's not real. It's all make-believe. So if you're looking at this thing like, oh, I'm going to be a Pinterest mom and you're stressing out because I don't have the money at the time, my kids are insane, there's chocolate sauce everywhere. I can't do that. No one can. That's other people, that's not us. Okay? So don't stress that out about that. In fact, the funniest thing, I was in my kids fourth grade class and you know when you're in a class and I always have sign-up sheets to come and help volunteer at the class and I was there taking my kid off and somebody, they had a sign-up sheet and nobody would sign it for the craft day. And someone's like, hey, aren't you the craft mom with the big craft log? And I was like, maybe why? And like, well, you should sign for the craft. You can do all the crafts for the kids. And I didn't want to, but I knew that I was the craft mom and I was like freaking out. And I was like, okay, sure. So I signed up and I literally went home for the next two weeks stressing out because I'm the worst least crafty person I've ever met. Yes, I run a craft blog, but I don't know how to do crafts. In fact, I hire people to make up crafts, to post on the blog because I don't know how to do crafts. And I stressed out and I'm seriously stressing out forever because of this. And then also I realized one day, excuse me, about a week into me stressing out, I had about a week before something happened and I was like, wait a minute. I don't have to actually do any of this stuff. So I literally went to my own blog. I scrolled through and I found this one. It was talking about paper plates that you could cut them out and make flowers. And so I went and bought a whole bunch of paper plates, brought scissors and we came in and I did it with the kids and the funniest thing. Look at these kids' flowers, bunch of pictures. Those are the kids' flowers. You want to see mine? Mine was the worst out of all of them. I'm the least crafty mom in the world yet I run one of the largest craft sites on earth. So you just understand like when you're seeing these things, these are people like me who are running businesses or showing those things. It's not a reflection of you. If your house doesn't look like that, that's okay. It's not your business. You shouldn't be stressed out about it. Just know that this process, it can be simple. Like you can have the success. You can do the craft, you can do things to your kids, but it doesn't mean you have to be a crafty person. Okay, so that's secret number one. Now I want to transition to secret number two. And this is the shortcut that lets you become a super mom in less than 10 minutes. I'm curious, how many of you guys here believe in shortcuts? How many of you guys think of shortcuts? Like, ah, that's kind of my net question. We're doing shortcuts. Okay, some people feel weird about it. If you think about this, everything that's awesome in our lives today are shortcuts. Right? Back in the day, if you want to communicate with a friend across the country, what would you do? You would like, well, a long time ago, you'd like have to write a letter. They'd give it to a guy on a horse on a pony and he'd run across and he'd hand it. Right? That took a long time to get a message. Okay, but people build shortcuts. So eventually some guy build a phone. You could pick up the phone, you could dial in, there was a shortcut. And how much better is that shortcut? Now I can talk to people really, really fast. The shortcuts aren't bad, they're really, really good. In fact, almost everything you do in your life right now is some kind of shortcut. The iPhones you're holding, the computers running, these are all shortcuts that were given to us to make things really, really easy. Okay, but for some reason as moms, we think we have to be martyrs. Like, oh, if I'm not sitting in the kitchen for 14 hours a day making muffins with my kids, I'm a bad mom. No, that's not true. Like, there's shortcuts that are out there for you. In fact, my entire blog has been this big, huge shortcut to help moms to do things really, really quick. And so the coolest thing to understand is that you as a mom don't have to worry about all these types of things and being creative and being out of the crafts. Like, all this has been done for you. The shortcut is in place. All you got to do is understand where to get it and then you just take it out, give it to your kids, and then now they're entertained. They can do all sorts of things. Okay, so don't think you have to go and be super creative, have time and energy, time and all that kind of stuff doesn't really matter. All you got to do is use the shortcuts. Okay, and your kids will not know that you downloaded it from me. They will not know that my designers create this thing to give to you. All they know is that you were the mom, you were their mom. The next morning you wake up and you're like, how do I recreate that yesterday? That was hard, right? It's tough. But with these shortcuts, you can do it every single day. It doesn't take all your time and your energy. It's really fun. So I'm going to go deep in these shortcuts here in a little bit, but they're awesome. And that brings me now to secret number three. And this is one that's really important because a lot of people who go into this, they understand that like, yes, I can do this. I can be a great mom. I can be the best, funniest mom ever. I think I can use these shortcuts to speed it up. But the problem they think is like, but my kids are insane. Like I can't even get them to sit for five seconds. Okay, at dinner time, I'm trying to eat. And like literally I'm feeding one kid and I turn to feed the other kid and that kid's gone already and I can't figure this out. And it's just like they're literally bouncing off the walls. And so I want to understand the crazier kids are the easiest actually works. Okay? In fact, there's something that I teach all the women that go through our programs this and that boredom is actually really, really good for your kids. Which is kind of backwards, right? Like most of us think if our kids bored, I need to entertain them quick and I take them on a plate and I call their friends and I give them an iPad. Like here's my iPad and like, and we're freaking out trying to keep them entertained all the time, but guess what happens? When they do that, they lose all of their ability to think and to create and to imagine. Just remember when we were kids, what do we do? We sit outside in the grass with a stick and we play for hours, sword fights, all sorts of stuff. And it was amazing. We were fine. But for some reason we think that our kids have to have a screen in front of their face or they're going to be bored. Guess what? Boredom's actually good. It's one of the best things you can have for your kids. So don't stress out about that. In fact, one of the things that's interesting is I watched the moms come through our programs and one of the biggest problems they have, and I want to teach this to you guys, because if you understand this, it's going to make life a lot easier, is that they have their kids and their kids are playing on iPads or iPhones or watching TV or whatever and they're doing that for three or four hours and they're zoning the zone. And then all of a sudden the mom feels guilt. Mom's like, okay, I need to get my kids bored. So they come in and they're like, turn the TV off and they're mad. They're like, turn the TV off. Take the kids outside, throw them out in the sun. And the kids are out in the sun and they're like, I can't see. It hurts. And they're freaked out and there's really weird thing happens. You have to understand that that's what causes a lot of these problems. The kids are like, stimulate, stimulate, stimulate. And they put it out here and they melt it in the grass and the sun's shining. And they know what to do and it's really, really hard. You have to understand there's a transition point. In fact, there's a story. Some of you guys have heard this before where they take a frog, right? And if you have this boiling water and you take a frog and you throw the frog in the boiling water, it jumps right out instantly, right? That's what literally happens to your kids. That's why when you try to break them off the iPads and stuff, it lasts like eight seconds and then they're back in freaking you out and driving you crazy and then you just like, fine, take the iPads, go away, right? That's what happens, okay? You just threw a frog into a boiling pit of water. Of course that happened. But check this out. If you take a pot of cold water and you put a frog in it, the frog will sit there and start swimming, do the backstroke and you turn the heat on. You'll sit there and get warmer. You get warmer, it'll hang out. It'll kill all the way till it actually cooks a frog. And then you can eat it if you eat frogs. If you don't eat frogs, that understands as well. It's a bad analogy for you. But if you do eat frogs, it's pretty cool. That's how we're going to see your kids. You have to understand how to transition them, okay? And so you think about that and we teach our moms a whole bunch of ways to do this. But you take your kids from whatever, from these high stimulus things and the goal is to get in the spot where they can be creative. But there's a transition point. How do we take them from this step to this step to this step to now they can actually do it and they can create? And that's the magic, okay? And again, it doesn't come back to you being an insanely amazing mom. It comes down to you understanding like, okay, I'm taking from here to here to here to here and that's how we boil a frog. That's how we save a kid. And that's the magic, okay? So these are some of the core things I want to share with you guys today. Now one other piece I want to tell you about my journey, I told you this a little earlier. Remember I told you as I started a blog, this craft blog? It was crazy about this blog. It became like my contribution in the world. It came the way that I could, this outlet I had to share ideas and things with people. And it's become a part of my life. And I've watched this, this has grown over the last few years. Right now we have over 3.2 million people who are fans on my fan page that come to our blog and it's crazy. And the blog has literally changed my life. Of everything I've done, that was the piece that I love the most. And a lot of people always come to me and say, hey, how did you become a blogger? How do you do a blogger? I want to do a blog. And what's funny is that first it's hard because usually your kids are so insane you can't do it. So after you go through this process with your kids and now you've got this rhythm and this system happening where they're taking care of, they're having fun, it's going to give you so much more free time. And for most of you guys, I know you're going to want to, the next step for you in this evolution is to start your own blog. Now I don't tell people this a lot because it's kind of private. I don't share it with most people at all. But I want to share with you guys because I want to open your eyes to what's actually possible. So I started this blog 10 years ago just as an outlet. Wrapping the presents all that kind of crap. I think the reason I most of us love Christmas is because typically the day after Thanksgiving what happens? We sit down with all of our kids and we pull out these boxes of all these things and we set up the Christmas tree, we set up the lights, we put up Santa, we put up Jesus in the manger, we do all these things, we decorate the house and we have this connection with our kids typically for three, four, five or six hours. We get the whole house prepared for Christmas and because that draws us all together and then that feeling, the Christmas feeling lasts the entire month, right? So people love Christmas so much. Then it ends, we take all the stuff down and then we're like miserable for the next 11 months. So by the time we're connecting as a family. So we decided to do that we did inside of our family.
